rationale_for_baseline_tier: A benign but severely symptomatic vesiculobullous tinea pedis — prompt prescription (short steroid + antifungal) and self-care with a secondary-infection safety-net; not an emergency.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Blister fluid turning to thick yellow pus, hot redness spreading up the ankle, or fever
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the blister fluid turns to thick yellow pus, the redness becomes hot and spreads up your ankle, or you develop a fever — go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise airing/drying and footwear treatment to prevent recurrence.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: These blisters are a reaction to athlete's-foot fungus. Use the strong steroid ointment on the blisters for 5 days and the antifungal cream on the whole foot daily for 4 weeks, keep your feet aired/dry, and don't pop the blisters. Watch for pus, spreading redness or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within days for prescription; urgent care/ED if secondary infection
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed unless purulent blisters/ascending redness/fever — then ED.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document vesiculobullous tinea pedis with onychomycosis, short potent steroid + whole-foot antifungal, footwear/drying advice, and an infection safety-net.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: No secondary-infection safety-net for blistered skin, or missing the fungal cause.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A secondary cellulitis develops in the blistered skin and is not recognised.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No treatment of the blistering/fungus, leaving severe pain and relapse.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency escalation for uncomplicated vesiculobullous tinea pedis.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot KOH-scrape remotely; but concurrent classic tinea features indicate the trigger.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the fungal cause (whole-foot antifungal) with a short steroid for the blistering, advise airing/drying and not popping blisters, and safety-net for infection.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Missing the fungal cause, or advising popping the blisters.
